project.name: taugo
project.description: Kernel exécutable Go de l'opérateur τ : décide d'un régime d'appel (Déterministe, Probabiliste ou Refus) à la frontière agentique sous cinq invariants réfutables (I1-I5), implémentant le chapitre III.8 de la monographie « Interopérabilité Agentique en Écosystème d'Entreprise » (v2.4.3). Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: Pipeline GitHub Actions principal de TauGo : matrice 3 OS (Linux/macOS/Windows), exécution -race via CGO sur Linux/macOS, lint golangci-lint et fuzz court 30 s sur I1-I5 — gate qualité avant merge.

nodes.name: coverage.yml
nodes.summary: Workflow GitHub Actions dédié à la couverture : applique le gate per-package ≥ 90 % sur internal/tau/* et ≥ 80 % global imposé par le PRD §15.3.

nodes.name: .golangci.yml
nodes.summary: Configuration golangci-lint épinglée v1.64.8 — 24 linters actifs (calque FibGo) régissant la qualité du code Go ; gouverne complexité, étanchéité Clean Architecture et anti-patrons.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Liste des chemins exclus du scanner Understand-Anything (artefacts de build, données binaires, dossiers générés) afin de cibler l'analyse sur le code de production.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Historique Keep-a-Changelog du projet TauGo couvrant les jalons v0.0.1-alpha à v0.1.0 puis v0.1.1-pre — trace du refactor consolidation post-audit et de la levée des sept anti-patrons.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Règles opérationnelles V0.4 pour les agents Claude travaillant sur TauGo : doctrine kernel-only, cartographie agent → rôle, sept anti-patrons gardés, étanchéité Clean Architecture et workflow git.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation file for the generate-corpus CLI — describes its role producing synthetic calibration corpora used to seed default thresholds.

nodes.name: generator.go
nodes.summary: Synthetic corpus generator producing reproducible JSONL entries (six branches: refus-frontiere, refus-I3, refus-I4, deterministe, probabiliste, hysteresis) seeded by a PCG RNG — deterministic byte-identical output across runs for a given seed.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses math/rand/v2 PCG generator for reproducible deterministic output; six distinct branches generate Exchange entries matching the five Refus paths plus the determ./prob. regimes.

nodes.name: AnnotatedEntry
nodes.summary: Wrapper carrying an Exchange plus the ExpectedRegime computed by an Annotator — produced by GenerateAnnotated for golden-corpus tests verifying that the kernel's actual decision matches the synthetic intent.

nodes.name: Annotator
nodes.summary: Narrow interface (Decide method only) abstracting the kernel so the generator can call it without depending on the orchestration package — typically satisfied by app.Dispatcher.

nodes.name: Generator
nodes.summary: Deterministic synthetic-corpus generator holding a seeded PCG RNG — exposes Generate (plain JSONL) and GenerateAnnotated (with expected regime) and six internal builders for the corpus branches.

nodes.name: GenerateAnnotated
nodes.summary: Generates n synthetic entries respecting the supplied DistributionProfile, runs each through the supplied Annotator to capture the expected regime, and emits one canonical JSON line per entry — used by golden tests to validate kernel determinism end-to-end.

nodes.name: Generate
nodes.summary: Generates n synthetic Exchange entries for the given DistributionProfile and writes them as JSONL — does not annotate so the byte-identical hash remains stable independent of kernel evolution.

nodes.name: buildPlan
nodes.summary: Builds the deterministic execution plan of n entries by partitioning according to branchWeights and absorbing rounding remainder on the dominant deterministe branch.

nodes.name: buildRefusFrontiere
nodes.summary: Synthesizes an Exchange satisfying at least one of the four classical frontier conditions — exercise path for the refus-frontiere branch of the kernel.

nodes.name: buildRefusI3
nodes.summary: Synthesizes an Exchange triggering the I3 ontological refus path (high D-AUTORITÉ score without AttestationInstitutionnelle, per Searle 1995).

nodes.name: buildRefusI4
nodes.summary: Synthesizes an Exchange triggering the I4 coherence-rupture refus path (s < θ_sens ∧ i ≥ θ_inv — incoherent contracts).

nodes.name: buildDeterministe
nodes.summary: Synthesizes an Exchange landing in the Deterministe regime — high-invariant, low-sens, attested authority.

nodes.name: buildProbabiliste
nodes.summary: Synthesizes a randomized Exchange landing in the Probabiliste regime using the generator's PCG RNG for slight per-entry variation.

nodes.name: buildHysteresis
nodes.summary: Synthesizes a borderline Exchange near the hysteresis band (V1 future-use) — currently classified as Probabiliste but reserved for V1.1 hysteresis tests (ADR-0007).

nodes.name: main_test.go
nodes.summary: Test suite covering the generate-corpus CLI: byte-wise reproducibility (frozen-hash golden), seed divergence, distribution balance, annotation correctness, and all CLI error paths (exit 2 for usage errors, exit 1 for I/O failures).

nodes.name: TestGenerateCorpus_FrozenHash_Seed42_120_Balanced
nodes.summary: Frozen-hash golden test asserting that Generate(seed=42, n=120, balanced) yields a SHA-256 byte-identical to the checked-in value — guards corpus determinism across Go versions and platforms.

nodes.name: TestGoldenCorpus_FrozenHash_Seed42_200_Balanced
nodes.summary: End-to-end golden test wiring GenerateAnnotated with a real app.NewDispatcher and comparing the produced JSONL hash to the checked-in synthetic-corpus-120-seed42-balanced.jsonl artefact.

nodes.name: TestGenerateCorpus_DistributionBalanced_RoughlyEven
nodes.summary: Statistical test verifying that the balanced DistributionProfile produces roughly even regime counts across all six branches (±25 % tolerance) — protects against silent distribution drift.

nodes.name: TestRun_WithAnnotateFlag
nodes.summary: CLI integration test exercising the --annotate flag, asserting that each produced entry carries a non-empty ExpectedRegime field populated by the kernel.

nodes.name: main.go
nodes.summary: Entry point of the generate-corpus binary — parses flags (count, seed, distribution, output, annotate), wires the Generator and optional app.Dispatcher annotator, and writes JSONL to stdout or file.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Thin shim that delegates to run() and exits with its status code — keeps the testable logic in run() to avoid os.Exit in tests.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Parses CLI flags, validates the requested distribution profile, wires the Generator (+ optional app.Dispatcher when --annotate), then emits JSONL to stdout or to the requested output file — returns exit codes 0 (ok), 2 (usage error), 1 (I/O failure).

nodes.name: synthetic-corpus-120-seed42-balanced.jsonl
nodes.summary: Frozen golden corpus of 120 synthetic entries (seed 42, balanced distribution) used as byte-identical baseline by the corpus reproducibility tests — modification requires an ADR.

nodes.name: calibrate_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the runCalibrate sub-command, parseDateRev helper and loadCorpus loader — covers happy-path exit 0, every usage-error exit-2 path, and I/O exit-1 failures.

nodes.name: TestRunCalibrate_HappyPath_Exit0
nodes.summary: Happy-path test for `tau calibrate`: feeds a temp corpus, asserts exit 0, and verifies the produced profile is non-empty JSON.

nodes.name: TestLoadCorpus_HappyPath
nodes.summary: Verifies that loadCorpus decodes a well-formed JSONL corpus into the expected number of CorpusEntry values.

nodes.name: writeTempCorpus
nodes.summary: Test helper that writes n minimal CorpusEntry JSONL records to a temp file and returns its path — reused across all calibrate test cases.

nodes.name: calibrate.go
nodes.summary: Implements the `tau calibrate` sub-command: parses flags (corpus, output, date-rev, seed, cpu-fingerprint), loads the JSONL corpus, calls calibration.Calibrate, and writes a canonical (byte-reproducible) Profile JSON.

nodes.name: runCalibrate
nodes.summary: Entry point of the calibrate sub-command — validates flag presence, parses date-rev (RFC3339 or YYYY-MM-DD), loads corpus, computes profile fingerprints (corpus + CPU), invokes calibration.Calibrate then writes the byte-canonical JSON profile.

nodes.name: parseDateRev
nodes.summary: Parses a date-revision string accepting either RFC3339 or bare YYYY-MM-DD form — returns time.Time used to set the Profile expiration boundary (anti-pattern #3 guard).

nodes.name: loadCorpus
nodes.summary: Streams a JSONL file into a slice of calibration.CorpusEntry using a streaming json.Decoder — returns wrapped error per offending line on decode failure.

nodes.name: decide_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the in-process runDecide function covering exit 0 on valid Exchange, exit 2 on invalid/empty JSON, output schema validity and Regime field population.

nodes.name: TestRunDecide_ValidExchange_RegimeIsSet
nodes.summary: Asserts that a valid Exchange piped through runDecide produces a Decision JSON with a non-empty Regime field — verifies the kernel's contract surface end-to-end.

nodes.name: main_test.go
nodes.summary: End-to-end test suite for the `tau` binary: TestMain rebuilds the binary in a temp dir, then runs sub-tests that exec the binary with stdin/stdout pipes to verify decide and calibrate behaviours.

nodes.name: TestEndToEnd_DecideDeterministe
nodes.summary: End-to-end test: feeds an Exchange JSON to the compiled `tau decide` binary and asserts that the emitted Decision carries Regime=Deterministe.

nodes.name: TestEndToEnd_DecideProbabiliste
nodes.summary: End-to-end test: feeds an Exchange JSON to the compiled `tau decide` binary and asserts the Probabiliste regime branch.

nodes.name: TestEndToEnd_Calibrate_ProducesValidProfile
nodes.summary: End-to-end calibrate test: exec'es the compiled `tau calibrate` binary twice against the same checked-in corpus and asserts byte-identical Profile output — guards calibration reproducibility (PRD §11.5).

nodes.name: buildAndRun
nodes.summary: TestMain helper that builds the `tau` binary into a temp directory and propagates the test exit code — keeps E2E tests hermetic by avoiding dependency on go-install state.

nodes.name: main.go
nodes.summary: Entry point of the `tau` CLI — dispatches to runDecide or runCalibrate, or prints usage. runDecide wires app.NewDispatcher (which injects calibration.DefaultProfile per ADR P0-02) to consume Exchange from stdin and emit Decision JSON.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Thin shim delegating to runMain with os.Args, os.Stdin/Stdout/Stderr — exits with the returned status code.

nodes.name: runMain
nodes.summary: Sub-command dispatcher routing to runDecide, runCalibrate, the version flag or printUsage — testable in-process variant of main accepting injected stdin/stdout/stderr.

nodes.name: runDecide
nodes.summary: Decodes one Exchange JSON value from stdin, calls app.NewDispatcher().Decide and writes the Decision JSON to stdout — exit codes: 0 success, 2 invalid JSON, 1 dispatch error.

nodes.name: printUsage
nodes.summary: Prints the canonical `tau` CLI usage block to the supplied writer — invoked on missing/unknown sub-command and on --help.

nodes.name: runmain_test.go
nodes.summary: In-process tests for runMain covering: no args → usage on stderr, unknown command → exit 2, --version → exit 0, decide via stdin → exit 0, calibrate via flags → exit 0.

nodes.name: go.mod
nodes.summary: Module manifest declaring `github.com/agbruneau/taugo` on Go 1.25 with no external dependencies — reflects the kernel's anti-platform discipline (PRD §3.3).

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: Cibles de build, test, lint, fuzz, benchmark, calibrate et e2e du projet — calque FibGo ; `make test` exécute -race via CGO, `make coverage` applique les gates per-package, `make build-reproducible` vérifie le byte-identique.

nodes.name: PRD.md
nodes.summary: Spécification canonique V0.3 de TauGo en cinq blocs (cadrage, théorie opérationnalisée, architecture, discipline d'exécution, programme) — source de vérité pour les invariants I1-I5, les trois dimensions D-SENS/D-AUTORITÉ/D-INVARIANT, le pseudo-algorithme à 8 étapes et les sept anti-patrons.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Document d'entrée du dépôt — doctrine kernel-only, anti-objectifs, quick start (build/test/calibrate), architecture des quatre couches, exemples d'usage CLI et intégration Go, état v0.1.1-pre et table des invariants.

nodes.name: anti_patterns_test.go
nodes.summary: Guards the seven CLAUDE.md anti-patterns by AST-walking the taugo packages: forbids exported Predict*/Expected*/Forecast* identifiers, asserts I3 perimption date stays in the future, and checks that unmodeled observations are surfaced in Trace summaries.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses go/parser and go/token at test time to walk the live source tree — a meta-test that operates on the AST of sibling packages.

nodes.name: TestNoPredictiveAPI
nodes.summary: AST-walks tau/orchestration/calibration/bridge packages and fails if any exported identifier matches the predictive name pattern (anti-pattern #1).

nodes.name: exportedDeclName
nodes.summary: Helper that extracts the exported name from an AST FuncDecl, TypeSpec, or ValueSpec, returning empty string for unexported declarations.

nodes.name: TestI3_DateRevisionRespectee
nodes.summary: Verifies that calibration.DefaultProfile().DateRevision stays beyond now and within the I3PerimptionLimite, guarding anti-pattern #3 (expired calibration profile).

nodes.name: TestUnmodeledObservationsReported
nodes.summary: Ensures Trace.Summary() reports unmodeled observations so silent gaps in the kernel cannot accumulate (anti-pattern #4).

nodes.name: agentmesh_test.go
nodes.summary: Exercises the AgentMeshKafka bridge translation (ToTauExchange) and streaming wrapper (StreamAsTauExchanges) — covers attestation preservation, discovery-mode mapping, error propagation, and context-cancel draining.

nodes.name: TestToTauExchange_Nominal
nodes.summary: Validates the canonical fields produced by ToTauExchange from a fully populated AgentMeshKafka envelope.

nodes.name: TestToTauExchange_NoAttestation
nodes.summary: Ensures absence of institutional attestation in the source envelope yields a nil Attestation field on the Exchange (precondition for I3 refusal).

nodes.name: TestToTauExchange_UnknownDiscoveryMode_FallsBackToZero
nodes.summary: Confirms that an unrecognized discovery-mode string degrades to the zero value rather than panicking or guessing.

nodes.name: TestToTauExchange_DiscoveryModeMapping
nodes.summary: Table-driven test mapping each AgentMeshKafka discovery-mode string to the corresponding tau.DiscoveryMode enum value.

nodes.name: TestToTauExchange_AttestationPreserved
nodes.summary: Checks that institutional attestation fields (issuer, signature timestamp) round-trip verbatim through the bridge translation.

nodes.name: blockingAdapter
nodes.summary: Test-only adapter that exposes pre-populated exchange and error channels to exercise concurrent draining behaviour of StreamAsTauExchanges.

nodes.name: TestStreamAsTauExchanges_DrainsErrsOnContextCancel
nodes.summary: Regression test asserting StreamAsTauExchanges drains the adapter error channel after context cancellation so the adapter goroutine can exit cleanly without deadlock.

nodes.name: TestStreamAsTauExchanges_RoundTrip3Lines
nodes.summary: End-to-end smoke test that streams three exchanges through the FileAdapter and verifies all are converted and forwarded in order.

nodes.name: TestStreamAsTauExchanges_PropagatesErrors
nodes.summary: Verifies that adapter-level errors are forwarded on the err channel rather than swallowed.

nodes.name: agentmesh.go
nodes.summary: Application-layer adapter that translates AgentMeshKafka envelopes into tau.Exchange and exposes StreamAsTauExchanges, a non-blocking pipeline with context-aware draining of adapter errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the boundary between non-deterministic external bus (Kafka via FileAdapter) and the deterministic tau kernel.

nodes.name: ToTauExchange
nodes.summary: Converts a single AgentMeshKafka exchange into the kernel's neutral tau.Exchange DTO, preserving attestation and mapping discovery mode.

nodes.name: StreamAsTauExchanges
nodes.summary: Wraps an AgentMeshKafka adapter's Stream into two channels of tau.Exchange and error, draining adapter errors so the underlying goroutine can shut down cleanly on context cancel or source close.

nodes.name: app_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests the lifecycle layer: NewDispatcher charges DefaultProfile, the perimption guard fires when the clock advances past DateRevision, and the LLM selection defaults to the deterministic stub.

nodes.name: TestApp_NewDispatcher_ChargeProfilParDefaut
nodes.summary: Asserts that app.NewDispatcher() injects calibration.DefaultProfile() so the CLI path benefits from perimption guard by default (P0-02, v0.1.1).

nodes.name: TestApp_NewDispatcher_PeremptionDetecteeAvecHorlogeSimulee
nodes.summary: Advances the dispatcher clock past Profile.DateRevision and verifies the kernel returns a Refusal with DiagPeremptionProfile diagnostic.

nodes.name: TestSelectLLM_RealBackend_Panics
nodes.summary: Confirms that setting TAUGO_LLM_BACKEND=real triggers a panic inside NewDispatcher until a real backend is wired.

nodes.name: TestDefaultLLMIsStub
nodes.summary: Confirms that the default LLM backend is the deterministic stub by comparing repeated Decide invocations on the same Exchange for byte-identical results.

nodes.name: app.go
nodes.summary: Application lifecycle entry point. NewDispatcher selects an LLM (stub by default, panics on TAUGO_LLM_BACKEND=real until wired), loads calibration.DefaultProfile, and builds the orchestration dispatcher — activating the perimption guard on the CLI path (P0-02 v0.1.1).
nodes.languageNotes: Sole allowed location (with bridge/llm) for concrete LLM imports per ADR-0003 and arch_test.go rule V-A2.

nodes.name: NewDispatcher
nodes.summary: Constructs an orchestration.Dispatcher wired with the selected LLM and calibration.DefaultProfile; panics if LLM selection fails.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation for internal/app: describes the lifecycle layer responsible for wiring concrete LLM, calibration profile, and orchestration dispatcher.

nodes.name: select_llm_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for selectLLM covering stub-provider happy path, empty provider defaulting to stub, unknown provider yielding a typed DispatchError, and real backend not-implemented error.

nodes.name: TestSelectLLM_StubProvider
nodes.summary: Verifies the stub provider name returns a non-nil bridge/llm.Stub client.

nodes.name: TestSelectLLM_ProviderInconnuRetourneErreur
nodes.summary: Asserts that an unknown provider name yields a *errors.DispatchError with the provider name embedded in the detail field.

nodes.name: arch_test.go
nodes.summary: Architectural etanchéité guard enforcing the seven Clean Architecture rules (tau/* cannot import orchestration/bridge, bridge cannot import tau directly, dimensions and invariants cannot cross-import, no concrete LLM outside app/ and bridge/llm/). Modifications require an ADR per CLAUDE.md.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses go/build + go/parser to walk imports at test time — the single source of truth for layer boundaries.

nodes.name: TestArchitectureLayering
nodes.summary: Walks each layer's imports and fails when any package imports from a deny-listed prefix (tau/* → orchestration, dimensions ↔ invariants, etc.).

nodes.name: TestBridgeNoTauImport
nodes.summary: Walks every .go file under internal/bridge and fails if any import path starts with the tau/* prefix, enforcing strict separation between bridges and the kernel.

nodes.name: TestArchNoConcreteLLMInDomain
nodes.summary: Walks tau/* and orchestration/* trees and fails on any of 12 substrings denoting concrete LLM SDKs (anthropic, openai, etc.) — guards anti-pattern #6 (v0.1.1).

nodes.name: client.go
nodes.summary: Defines the narrow LLM Client interface (Fingerprint + Interpret) that confines non-determinism away from the tau kernel per ADR-0003.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-method interface follows ISP — the only contract the kernel sees of any LLM backend.

nodes.name: Client
nodes.summary: Interface contract for any LLM backend used through the bridge: Fingerprint identifies the model, Interpret returns a deterministic intent score for a payload.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation for bridge/llm: documents the boundary role of the package and the stub-by-default policy.

nodes.name: stub_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the deterministic Stub LLM client: Fingerprint stability across calls and Interpret returning bounded, reproducible values for identical inputs.

nodes.name: stub.go
nodes.summary: Deterministic stub implementation of the LLM Client interface. Returns a stable fingerprint and a value-bounded Interpret score derived hash-style from intent length — used by all tests and as the safe default backend.
nodes.languageNotes: Hash-based scoring keeps output deterministic — anti-pattern #6 mitigation lives here.

nodes.name: Stub
nodes.summary: Empty struct implementing the deterministic LLM Client used by default in tests and CI.

nodes.name: errors_test.go
nodes.summary: Covers DispatchError, RefusError, and CalibrationError types: message formatting in nominal and detail-less cases, Unwrap propagation through errors.Is, and identification of the Err* sentinels (ADR-0009).

nodes.name: TestDispatchError_ErrorMessage
nodes.summary: Sub-tests verifying the formatted error message of DispatchError in both nominal and detail-less variants.

nodes.name: TestCalibrationError_ErrorMessage
nodes.summary: Sub-tests verifying CalibrationError formatting with and without an underlying cause.

nodes.name: TestSentinels_IdentifieesViaErrorsIs
nodes.summary: Table-driven check that each ErrFrontiereFranchie / ErrVerrouOntologique / ErrPeremptionProfile / ErrIncoherenceI4 sentinel is identifiable via errors.Is.

nodes.name: errors.go
nodes.summary: Typed error package per ADR-0009: defines *DispatchError, *RefusError, *CalibrationError with Error/Unwrap methods, plus the canonical Err* sentinels mapped to refusal diagnostics.
nodes.languageNotes: Error types follow Go 1.13+ errors.Is/As idiom via Unwrap so callers can pattern-match without string comparison.

nodes.name: DispatchError
nodes.summary: Typed error wrapping a dispatcher-step failure with Stage, Cause, ExchangeID, and optional Detail; supports errors.Unwrap.

nodes.name: RefusError
nodes.summary: Typed error representing a first-rank kernel refusal carrying the originating Stage, Diagnostic constant, and ExchangeID.

nodes.name: CalibrationError
nodes.summary: Typed error wrapping a calibration-profile failure with the offending ProfileVersion and underlying cause; supports errors.Unwrap.

nodes.name: builders_test.go
nodes.summary: Validates that BuildExchange returns sane defaults and that every Option helper (WithID, WithAttestation, WithContext, …) applies its field as expected.

nodes.name: TestBuildExchange_Defaults
nodes.summary: Verifies that BuildExchange() with no options yields a fully populated Exchange with stable default ID, intent description, and discovery timestamp.

nodes.name: TestBuildExchange_AppliqueOptions
nodes.summary: Applies every Option helper at once and asserts each corresponding field is overridden on the resulting Exchange.

nodes.name: builders.go
nodes.summary: Test-only Exchange builder using the functional Options pattern. Eight With* helpers and BuildExchange produce reproducible tau.Exchange fixtures with sensible defaults — preferred entry point per CLAUDE.md.
nodes.languageNotes: Options pattern (closures over an *Exchange) avoids constructor explosion as Exchange grows.

nodes.name: WithContext
nodes.summary: Option helper that lazily initializes the Exchange Context map before setting key→value, avoiding nil-map panics.

nodes.name: BuildExchange
nodes.summary: Constructs a default tau.Exchange and applies the provided variadic Options. Single source of truth for test exchanges per CLAUDE.md anti-construction rule.

nodes.name: thresholds_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests the Thresholds value type: zero-value behaviour, field-level accessors, and the Ordered() invariant ensuring Deterministe < Probabiliste < AuthBlock.

nodes.name: thresholds.go
nodes.summary: Transverse value-type package per ADR-0006: Thresholds struct holding the six regime cutoffs (Deterministe, Probabiliste, AuthBlock, SensCoherence, InvCoherence, HysteresisGap) plus an Ordered() guard. Imports no taugo package — strict downward etanchéité.
nodes.languageNotes: Zero-dep design enforced by arch_test.go — any taugo import here would break ADR-0006.

nodes.name: Thresholds
nodes.summary: Value-type struct grouping the six dispatcher thresholds shared between tau, orchestration, and calibration. Ordered() returns true when the cutoffs respect the regime hierarchy.

nodes.name: diagnostics.go
nodes.summary: Canonical Diag* string constants surfaced by Decide as Decision.Diagnostic — DiagFrontiereFranchie, DiagVerrouOntologique, DiagPeremptionProfile, DiagIncoherenceI4 — resolving literal duplication across the kernel and dispatcher.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure constant block; no functions or types. Centralizes refus diagnostic strings consumed across packages.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation for tau — the kernel CŒUR exposing Kernel.Decide(ctx, Exchange) -> Decision and the public types (Exchange, Decision, Trace, Regime).

nodes.name: operator.go
nodes.summary: Public surface of the tau kernel: defines all canonical types (Exchange, Attestation, Principal, Capability, Score, Trace, Decision, Kernel) plus Regime and DiscoveryMode enums with JSON encoders that accept both PascalCase strings (v0.1.1+) and legacy int (v0.1.0).
nodes.languageNotes: Trace exposes ventilated *Score fields (DSens, DAuthority, DInvariant) per ADR-0008 in addition to the legacy proxy TauScore. Evolution = potential API break.

nodes.name: operator_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests Regime/DiscoveryMode String() canonical forms, MarshalJSON returning PascalCase strings, UnmarshalJSON accepting both string and int (legacy corpus v0.1.0), and Trace.MarshalJSON omitempty on empty ventilated scores.

nodes.name: frontier.go
nodes.summary: First-rank guard FrontierCheck encoding the four classical conditions (UniversOuvert, CompositionVariable, PairProbabiliste, CoutNonBorne); Inside() returns true only when ALL four are violated. Bypass is anti-pattern #2.

nodes.name: frontier_test.go
nodes.summary: Guard tests for FrontierCheck.Inside() — TestFrontierCheck_Inside_AllConditionsViolated and TestFrontierCheck_Inside_OneConditionMet_Refused enforce anti-pattern #2 (no bypass of the four classical conditions).

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation for dimensions — encodes the three orthogonal dimensions D-SENS, D-AUTORITÉ, D-INVARIANT per chap. III.8.4. arch_test.go enforces no import to/from invariants.

nodes.name: score.go
nodes.summary: Shared package-private clamp01 helper that normalizes a float into [0,1] and substitutes 0 for NaN — used by all three dimension scorers to enforce the [0,1] codomain.

nodes.name: score_test.go
nodes.summary: Exhaustive clamp01 unit tests covering below-zero, above-one, exact bounds, and NaN substitution — five parallel TestClamp01_* cases.

nodes.name: dsens.go
nodes.summary: D-SENS scorer (where sense fixes) — exports SensWeights, DefaultSensWeights, ScoreDSens; combines four probes (Contract, RuntimeResolve, CapabilityDiscov, ReasonerIntent) and is the only dimension probing the injected llm.Client for reasoner intent extraction.
nodes.languageNotes: Only dimension that touches the LLM bridge (probeReasonerIntent calls c.Interpret); concrete LLM imports remain forbidden by arch_test.go — only the llm.Client interface is allowed.

nodes.name: dsens_test.go
nodes.summary: D-SENS unit tests covering bounds, static-vs-dynamic ordering, weights-sum-to-one, probe map population, default weights, empty intent, and LLM client error propagation through a local errClient stub.

nodes.name: dauthority.go
nodes.summary: D-AUTORITÉ scorer (reach of the delegation chain) — exports AuthorityWeights, DefaultAuthorityWeights, ScoreDAuthority; combines four probes (ChainDepth, CrossOrg, HumanAnchor, DynamicResolution) on Principal and Capability into a [0,1] score.

nodes.name: dauthority_test.go
nodes.summary: D-AUTORITÉ unit tests covering bounds, short-chain < long-chain monotonicity, probe-weights sum, probe map population, default weights structure, chain-depth saturation, and empty-organization edge case via testutil.BuildExchange.

nodes.name: dinvariant.go
nodes.summary: D-INVARIANT scorer (support of integration invariants) — exports InvariantWeights, DefaultInvariantWeights, ScoreDInvariant; combines four probes (EventRegistry, IdempotencyDerived, CapabilityMediation, EnumeratedPlan) into a [0,1] score.

nodes.name: dinvariant_test.go
nodes.summary: D-INVARIANT unit tests covering bounds, frozen < traced support ordering, probe-weights sum, probe map population, default weights, explicit enumerated plan, and capability-mediation under dynamic discovery.

nodes.name: Exchange
nodes.summary: The interoperability exchange submitted to τ — bundles initiator Principal, target Capability, intent description, discovery timestamp, optional institutional Attestation, and arbitrary context map. Carries the FrontierCheck() method.

nodes.name: Attestation
nodes.summary: Opposable reference populating the 'execution' pole of D-AUTORITÉ (chap. III.8.4.2.bis, Searle 1995) — emitter, reference, marker, assertion timestamp.

nodes.name: Principal
nodes.summary: Identity of the exchange initiator — ID, HumanInLoop flag, Organization, DelegationDepth — feeds the D-AUTORITÉ chain-depth and human-anchor probes.

nodes.name: Capability
nodes.summary: Target capability invoked at the boundary — ID, DiscoveryMode (Static/Dynamic/Unknown), ContractURI — feeds the D-SENS contract and discovery probes.

nodes.name: Score
nodes.summary: Per-dimension scoring envelope — Value in [0,1], named Probes map, Weights map, and ComputedAt timestamp. Returned by ScoreDSens / ScoreDAuthority / ScoreDInvariant.

nodes.name: Trace
nodes.summary: Ventilated decision trace (ADR-0008) — ExchangeID, legacy TauScore proxy, Frontier snapshot, Thresholds applied, unmodeled observations, duration, and the per-dimension *Score pointers (DSens, DAuthority, DInvariant) populated by the dispatcher at steps 2/4 and read by EvaluateI3/I4.

nodes.name: Decision
nodes.summary: Final τ output — Regime, Diagnostic string (one of Diag* constants), ProfileVersion, DateRevision, and embedded Trace. Single sink of all reasons and instrumentation.

nodes.name: Kernel
nodes.summary: The kernel contract — single-method interface Decide(ctx, Exchange) Decision. Only public output of the package; all behavior is funneled through this method.

nodes.name: UnmarshalJSON
nodes.summary: Regime JSON decoder accepting PascalCase strings (preferred, v0.1.1+) with case-insensitive fallback, then ints (legacy v0.1.0 corpus rétro-compat). Returns typed error on unknown value.

nodes.name: UnmarshalJSON
nodes.summary: DiscoveryMode JSON decoder — like Regime.UnmarshalJSON, accepts PascalCase strings with case-insensitive normalization (whitespace and underscores stripped, lowercased) plus legacy int fallback.

nodes.name: FrontierCheck
nodes.summary: Snapshot of the four classical-conditions probes: UniversOuvert, CompositionVariable, PairProbabiliste, CoutNonBorne. Inside() returns true only when ALL four are violated.

nodes.name: SensWeights
nodes.summary: Weights for the four D-SENS probes (Contract, RuntimeResolve, CapabilityDiscov, ReasonerIntent); must sum to 1.0 — checked at calibration time.

nodes.name: ScoreDSens
nodes.summary: Computes D-SENS [0,1] from four weighted probes (Contract, RuntimeResolve, CapabilityDiscov, ReasonerIntent). Only scorer that takes ctx and an injected llm.Client — propagates LLM errors verbatim.

nodes.name: AuthorityWeights
nodes.summary: Weights for the four D-AUTORITÉ probes (ChainDepth, CrossOrg, HumanAnchor, DynamicResolution); must sum to 1.0 — checked at calibration time.

nodes.name: ScoreDAuthority
nodes.summary: Computes D-AUTORITÉ [0,1] from four weighted probes (ChainDepth, CrossOrg, HumanAnchor, DynamicResolution); ctx-aware signature mirrors the other dimension scorers though no I/O is performed.

nodes.name: InvariantWeights
nodes.summary: Weights for the four D-INVARIANT probes (EventRegistry, IdempotencyDerived, CapabilityMediation, EnumeratedPlan); must sum to 1.0 — checked at calibration time.

nodes.name: ScoreDInvariant
nodes.summary: Computes D-INVARIANT [0,1] from four weighted probes (EventRegistry, IdempotencyDerived, CapabilityMediation, EnumeratedPlan); ctx-aware signature for symmetry, no I/O performed.

nodes.name: TestDAuthority_ShortChainLowerThanLong
nodes.summary: Monotonicity guard: a short delegation chain must score strictly lower D-AUTORITÉ than a long cross-org one — protects the chain-depth probe semantics from silent regression.

nodes.name: TestDInvariant_FrozenLowerThanTraced
nodes.summary: Monotonicity guard: a frozen-support exchange must score strictly lower D-INVARIANT than a fully traced one — protects the integration-support probe semantics from regression.

nodes.name: TestDSens_StaticLowerThanDynamic
nodes.summary: Monotonicity guard: a static-discovery exchange must score strictly lower D-SENS than a dynamic one — protects the runtime-resolve probe semantics from regression.

nodes.name: TestDSens_LLMClientError
nodes.summary: Verifies that an llm.Client returning an error propagates verbatim through ScoreDSens — exercises a local errClient stub that satisfies the bridge interface.

nodes.name: TestExchange_FrontierCheck_Reproduit
nodes.summary: Reproduces the FrontierCheck snapshot from a synthetic Exchange across multiple cases; cross-validates the four classical-condition booleans against Inside() to guarantee no silent bypass (anti-pattern #2).

nodes.name: TestFrontierCheck_Inside_OneConditionMet_Refused
nodes.summary: Verifies Inside() returns false whenever any one of the four classical conditions is met (universOuvert/compositionVariable/pairProbabiliste/coutNonBorne) — direct anti-pattern #2 garde.

nodes.name: TestRegime_UnmarshalJSON_AccepteStringEtInt
nodes.summary: Covers Regime JSON parsing for PascalCase strings, lowercase strings (legacy), int values (v0.1.0 corpus rétro-compat), and rejection of unknown strings or malformed JSON.

nodes.name: TestDiscoveryMode_UnmarshalJSON_AccepteStringEtInt
nodes.summary: Covers DiscoveryMode JSON parsing — PascalCase strings, whitespace/underscore-tolerant lowercase, int rétro-compat, and rejection of unknown values or malformed JSON.

nodes.name: TestTrace_MarshalJSON_ScoresVentilesOmitemptySiVides
nodes.summary: Verifies Trace JSON encoding — ventilated *Score fields (DSens/DAuthority/DInvariant) are omitted when nil (ADR-0008) and emitted when present, preserving wire-compat with v0.1.0 traces.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package godoc for `invariants`, documenting the five refutable invariants I1-I5 of operator τ as specified in chap. III.8.5.

nodes.name: evaluator.go
nodes.summary: Aggregates the five invariant evaluators (I1-I5) into a single `Statuses` struct exposing `AnyViolated` and `Summary` helpers used by the dispatcher for diagnostic emission.
nodes.languageNotes: `Status` enum with `String()` formatter; `Statuses` struct holds one field per invariant for stable diagnostic ordering.

nodes.name: evaluator_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the `Statuses` aggregator: zero-value behaviour, `AnyViolated`, ordered `Summary` output, `Status.String` enum and panic-safety of `EvaluateInvariants` on a zero Exchange.

nodes.name: fuzz_targets_test.go
nodes.summary: Hosts the five Go-native fuzz targets `FuzzI1_Conservation` … `FuzzI5_CompositionConjonctive` covering every refutable invariant of chap. III.8.5, each seeded with three deterministic corpora and pinned to a fixed reference clock via `fuzzRefTime`.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `testing.F.Add`/`Fuzz` API (Go 1.18+); shared `makeFuzzExchange` builder reduces per-target boilerplate.

nodes.name: i1_conservation.go
nodes.summary: Encodes invariant I1 (chap. III.8.5) — τ conserves the magnitude: it moves `t_fix` without altering content. Status: Probable, fuzzed ~8.6M exec/s.

nodes.name: i1_conservation_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for I1: identity trace conservation, drift detection on Exchange ID mutation, and behaviour of `EvaluateI1` across nominal, frontier-refusal and trace-drift cases.

nodes.name: i2_irreductibility.go
nodes.summary: Encodes invariant I2 (chap. III.8.5) — migrating residue is non-empty and not offline-rewireable. Status: Confirmé, fuzzed ~8.6M exec/s. Exposes `Residu`, `Recablage` and `EvaluateI2`.

nodes.name: i2_irreductibility_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for I2: residue non-emptiness on dynamic exchanges, frontier loss when all residual is removed via `Recablage`, and held/violated/not-applicable transitions of `EvaluateI2`.

nodes.name: i3_authority_asymmetry.go
nodes.summary: Encodes invariant I3 (chap. III.8.5) — institutional fact asymmetry per Searle 1995; without `AttestationInstitutionnelle` triggers ontological refus. Reads ventilated D-AUTHORITY score from `Decision.Trace` (ADR-0008). Status: Probable.
nodes.languageNotes: `I3PerimptionLimite()` is a getter (v0.1.1 — converted from package global to avoid anti-pattern #7), pinned to 2027-01-01. `EvaluateI3WithClock` allows injectable time for testing.

nodes.name: i3_authority_asymmetry_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for I3: profile-expiry detection (`IsProfileExpired`), nominal hold, expired-but-not-refused violation, expiry refusal hold, auth-block without attestation, auth-blocked refusal, other-refus not-applicable and probabiliste-below-θ_auth_block hold.

nodes.name: i4_coherence.go
nodes.summary: Encodes invariant I4 (chap. III.8.5) — coherence constraint `i ≈ pendant ⟹ s ≈ pendant`; detects silent rupture via per-dimension scores (chap. III.7). Reads ventilated D-SENS/D-INVARIANT from `Decision.Trace` (ADR-0008). Status: Hypothèse.

nodes.name: i4_coherence_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for I4: table-driven `IsIncoherent` over the (s, i, sens-coherence, inv-coherence) lattice, nominal hold, refusal-for-I4 hold, incoherent-non-refused violation, and other-refusal not-applicable cases.

nodes.name: i5_composition.go
nodes.summary: Encodes invariant I5 (chap. III.8.5) — composition conjonctive: stack inherits the conjunction and `M(π) ≥ max(|Aᵢ|)`. Status: Probable. Exposes `Aggregate`, `BoundsHold` (v0.1.1 optim −46% ns/op) and `EvaluateI5`.

nodes.name: i5_composition_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests and benchmarks for I5: empty stack, single layer, stable-sorted union, deduplication of blind spots, lower bound `M(π) ≥ max|Aᵢ|`, upper bound `M(π) ≤ Σ|Aᵢ|`, V1 pipeline hold, and `BenchmarkI5_{Aggregate,BoundsHold}`.

nodes.name: Statuses
nodes.summary: Composite holding the per-invariant `Status` (I1-I5) emitted by `EvaluateInvariants`. Provides `AnyViolated` and ordered `Summary` for diagnostic propagation.

nodes.name: EvaluateInvariants
nodes.summary: Runs `EvaluateI1`..`EvaluateI5` over `(Exchange, Decision)` and returns the aggregated `Statuses`. Called by the orchestration dispatcher after frontier check.

nodes.name: Summary
nodes.summary: Builds an ordered, short string summary of the five invariant statuses for inclusion in `Decision.Trace`.

nodes.name: Conserve
nodes.summary: Predicate enforcing I1 — checks that `Decision.Trace` preserves `Exchange.ID` and intent identity (τ moves `t_fix`, not content).

nodes.name: EvaluateI1
nodes.summary: Returns the I1 `Status`: NotApplicable on `RegimeRefus` due to frontier, Held when `Conserve` holds, Violated otherwise.

nodes.name: Residu
nodes.summary: Computes the migrating residue of an Exchange — the named features whose removal would push it outside the τ frontier.

nodes.name: Recablage
nodes.summary: Simulates offline rewiring by removing named residual features from an Exchange and re-running the frontier check; underpins the I2 non-rewireability assertion.

nodes.name: EvaluateI2
nodes.summary: Returns the I2 `Status`: Held when residue is non-empty and removal of all residuals would lose the frontier, NotApplicable on frontier refus, Violated otherwise.

nodes.name: I3PerimptionLimite
nodes.summary: Returns the I3 hard expiry date (2027-01-01 UTC). Converted from a package global to a getter in v0.1.1 to discharge anti-pattern #7 (mutable globals in `tau/*`).

nodes.name: IsProfileExpired
nodes.summary: Returns true when the Decision's calibration `DateRevision` is non-zero and has been crossed by the provided `now`. Implements anti-pattern #3 detection.

nodes.name: EvaluateI3WithClock
nodes.summary: Clock-injectable I3 evaluator. Reads ventilated D-AUTHORITY score from `Decision.Trace` (ADR-0008); checks ontological lock without `AttestationInstitutionnelle` and profile peremption. Returns Held / Violated / NotApplicable.

nodes.name: EvaluateI3
nodes.summary: Production entry point for I3 evaluation — delegates to `EvaluateI3WithClock(time.Now)`.

nodes.name: IsIncoherent
nodes.summary: Pure predicate detecting I4 incoherence: returns true when the invariant constraint `i ≈ pendant` holds but the sens constraint diverges. Uses per-dimension scores (chap. III.7).

nodes.name: EvaluateI4
nodes.summary: Returns the I4 `Status` by reading ventilated D-SENS and D-INVARIANT from `Decision.Trace` (ADR-0008) and feeding `IsIncoherent`. Held on refusal-for-I4, NotApplicable on other refusals.

nodes.name: Aggregate
nodes.summary: Stable-sorted, deduplicated union of blind-spot identifiers across a pile (stack) of layers — basis for the conjunction inheritance of I5.

nodes.name: BoundsHold
nodes.summary: Asserts the I5 bounds `max(|Aᵢ|) ≤ |M(π)| ≤ Σ|Aᵢ|` on a pile. Optimised −46% ns/op in v0.1.1 (PRD §6 cell I5).

nodes.name: EvaluateI5
nodes.summary: Returns the I5 `Status` for the V1 pipeline (single-layer hold by construction). Multi-layer support deferred to V2 per chap. III.8.5.

nodes.name: FuzzI1_Conservation
nodes.summary: Native Go fuzz target for invariant I1. Synthesises an Exchange from three bytes via `makeFuzzExchange` and asserts `EvaluateI1` never panics nor crosses to a Violated status without trace drift. Throughput ~8.6M exec/s.

nodes.name: FuzzI2_Irreductibilite
nodes.summary: Native Go fuzz target for invariant I2. Validates that for inside-frontier exchanges the residue is non-empty and removing every residual feature via `Recablage` always loses the frontier. Throughput ~8.6M exec/s.

nodes.name: FuzzI3_AsymetrieAutorite
nodes.summary: Native Go fuzz target for invariant I3. Uses `fuzzRefTime` clock to test authority-score / attestation / profile-expiry permutations through `EvaluateI3WithClock` and `IsProfileExpired`. Throughput ~8.2M exec/s.

nodes.name: FuzzI4_CoherenceContrainte
nodes.summary: Native Go fuzz target for invariant I4. Sweeps the 4-byte (s, i, sens-coherence, inv-coherence) tuple to assert `IsIncoherent` matches the pendant constraint asymmetry. Throughput ~9.5M exec/s.

nodes.name: FuzzI5_CompositionConjonctive
nodes.summary: Native Go fuzz target for invariant I5. Generates a multi-layer pile of varying width and asserts `BoundsHold` against `Aggregate`. Throughput ~1.1M exec/s; v0.1.1 optimisation cut `BoundsHold` by 46% ns/op.

nodes.name: calibrate_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the calibration grid-search, canonical JSON serialization, and CorpusEntry validation including the v0.1.1 ExpectedRegime -> LabeledRegime migration. Verifies determinism, byte-identical reproducibility, and rejection of invalid regime values.
nodes.languageNotes: All tests call t.Parallel(); migration tests verify rétro-compat per v0.1.1 corpus schema change.

nodes.name: calibrate.go
nodes.summary: Defines the calibration corpus loader, grid-search Calibrate routine, and the byte-identical canonical JSON (sorted-keys + LF) marshalling required for reproducible profile storage. CorpusEntry.Validate enforces score bounds and migrates legacy ExpectedRegime into LabeledRegime (v0.1.1).
nodes.languageNotes: Hand-rolled MarshalJSON for the kv pair preserves canonical key ordering even when nested arrays and objects mix.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation comment for internal/calibration -- declares the package's role as the profile, weights, drift, and atomic-threshold container.

nodes.name: drift_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests drift detection across CPU/LLM/corpus fingerprints and DateRevision expiry, plus stability of FingerprintCorpus SHA-256 and non-empty FingerprintCPU. Guards anti-pattern #3 (silently expired profile).

nodes.name: drift.go
nodes.summary: Detects drift between a stored calibration Profile and the current environment by comparing CPU, LLM, and corpus SHA-256 fingerprints plus DateRevision expiry. CheckDrift aggregates mismatch criteria for the dispatcher's freshness guard (anti-pattern #3).

nodes.name: profile_test.go
nodes.summary: Validates DefaultProfile invariants: non-empty fields, DateRevision at least 6 months in the future, weights sum to 1.0, and threshold ordering (deterministe > probabiliste).

nodes.name: profile.go
nodes.summary: Defines the Profile and Weights structs serialized as the calibration manifest, plus DefaultProfile() which is injected by app.NewDispatcher (v0.1.1 P0-02) to activate the périmption guard on the CLI default path.
nodes.languageNotes: DefaultProfile sets DateRevision two years out with VersionMonographie pinned to the chap. III.8 anchor.

nodes.name: store_test.go
nodes.summary: Covers Store.Save/Load roundtrips, byte-identical re-serialization across stores, current.json pointer updates, and OS-level failure modes (mkdir, writefile, refresh, corrupt JSON, SHA-256 export).

nodes.name: store_unix_test.go
nodes.summary: Unix-only test verifying Store.Save creates a symlink at current.json pointing to the latest profile manifest (gated by build tag).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses //go:build !windows tag to guard symlink semantics divergent from Windows.

nodes.name: store_windows_test.go
nodes.summary: Windows-only test verifying Store.Save falls back to a regular file copy when symlinks are unavailable on the target FS (gated by build tag).

nodes.name: store.go
nodes.summary: Filesystem-backed Profile store with versioned manifest paths, a current.json pointer (symlink on Unix, copy fallback on Windows), and SHA-256 export for reproducible-build verification.

nodes.name: drift-corpus-a.jsonl
nodes.summary: Tiny JSONL corpus fixture A used by FingerprintCorpus drift tests to assert SHA-256 stability and distinct hashes across content.

nodes.name: drift-corpus-b.jsonl
nodes.summary: Tiny JSONL corpus fixture B paired with corpus A to verify FingerprintCorpus produces distinct hashes for distinct content.

nodes.name: mini-corpus.jsonl
nodes.summary: 30-entry mini corpus of CorpusEntry rows consumed by calibrate_test.go to exercise grid-search determinism and the agreement-improving property.

nodes.name: thresholds_atomic_test.go
nodes.summary: Validates AtomicThresholds roundtrip, ordering invariant (panics on violation), SetTuning visibility, and concurrent-read safety using atomic Int64 storage.

nodes.name: thresholds_atomic.go
nodes.summary: Lock-free AtomicThresholds container backed by sync/atomic.Int64 fields (milli-encoded), exposing per-threshold getters, a Snapshot view, and SetTuning that re-validates the deterministe > probabiliste ordering invariant.
nodes.languageNotes: Stores floats as int64 millis via millis/fromMillis helpers so atomic ops apply; panics on construction with a violated ordering to fail fast.

nodes.name: weights_test.go
nodes.summary: Verifies CalibrateWeights V1 passthrough semantics, sum-to-one preservation, the WeightHook customization seam, and that the default strategy is V1.

nodes.name: weights.go
nodes.summary: Defines the WeightHook seam and the V1 CalibrateWeights implementation -- currently a passthrough returning the base Weights unchanged, awaiting a future learning strategy.

nodes.name: decision_invariants_test.go
nodes.summary: Cross-cutting tests asserting that every Decision carries a non-nil Trace, every Refus carries a non-empty diagnostic, and that returned Traces are isolated from caller mutation (defensive copy).

nodes.name: dispatcher_expiry_test.go
nodes.summary: Step-3 expiry guard tests: expired profile -> Refus(DiagPeremptionProfile); not-expired proceeds; zero DateRevision skips; nil profile skips. Uses WithClock for deterministic time injection.

nodes.name: dispatcher_invariants_test.go
nodes.summary: Step-8 invariant evaluation tests: no violation -> empty trace; violation -> trace enriched with diagnostic; regime unchanged by I1-I5 evaluation (observation, not decision).

nodes.name: dispatcher_profile_test.go
nodes.summary: Verifies Decision carries Profile version and DateRevision through the trace, and that NewDispatcherWithProfile applies the Profile.Weights at step 6 (v0.1.1).

nodes.name: dispatcher_scores_test.go
nodes.summary: Verifies Trace.{DSens,DAuthority,DInvariant} scores are populated by the dispatcher and that EvaluateI3/EvaluateI4 read the ventilated scores rather than the legacy TauScore proxy (ADR-0008).

nodes.name: dispatcher_test.go
nodes.summary: Core dispatcher unit tests with shared exchange builders (newExchangeInsideFrontier, newDeterministeExchange, newHysteresisExchange) and a fakeLLM stub. Covers Deterministe, Probabiliste, and hysteresis fall-through cases.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines the fakeLLM stub reused by sibling test files in the package.

nodes.name: dispatcher.go
nodes.summary: Implements the 8-step decision pseudo-algorithm (PRD §10): frontier check (step 1) → D-Authority + verrou ontologique (step 2) → profile expiry guard (step 3) → D-Sens + D-Invariant + I4 coherence (step 4) → composite TauScore via Profile.Weights (steps 5-6) → regime mapping with hysteresis (step 7) → invariants I1-I5 trace enrichment (step 8). Step order is not arbitrary.
nodes.languageNotes: WithClock seam enables deterministic time injection in expiry tests; dimensionWeights reads from Profile when present, else falls back to defaults.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation comment for internal/orchestration -- declares the package's role as the 8-step Decide pipeline orchestrator above the tau kernel.

nodes.name: guards_test.go
nodes.summary: Guard tests for the two non-bypassable refus paths: I3 verrou ontologique (high D-Authority without AttestationInstitutionnelle -> Refus, passes with attestation) and I4 incoherence (s < θ_sens and i ≥ θ_inv -> Refus).

nodes.name: thresholds.go
nodes.summary: Provides DefaultThresholds() returning the canonical thresholds.Ordered value used by NewDispatcher when no profile is injected. Aliases the transverse internal/thresholds type per ADR-0006.

nodes.name: CorpusEntry
nodes.summary: Calibration corpus row carrying the three dimension scores plus context flags (HumanInLoop, HasAttestation) and the expected/labeled regime ground truth. Validate enforces score bounds; migrate maps legacy ExpectedRegime to LabeledRegime (v0.1.1).

nodes.name: LoadCorpus
nodes.summary: Streams a JSONL corpus file from disk, decoding one CorpusEntry per line, applying schema migration, and validating each entry. Returns an error on malformed JSON or invalid fields.

nodes.name: Calibrate
nodes.summary: Performs a deterministic grid search over the threshold space against the labeled corpus, returning the Profile that maximizes agreement count. Calls CalibrateWeights (V1 passthrough) to keep weights stable in this version.

nodes.name: MarshalCanonical
nodes.summary: Serializes a Profile to canonical JSON (recursively sorted keys, indented, escape-HTML off, trailing newline) so that two identical Profile values produce byte-identical bytes -- foundational for reproducible-build SHA-256 verification.

nodes.name: UnmarshalCanonical
nodes.summary: Reverse of MarshalCanonical: decodes canonical JSON bytes back into a Profile with a wrapped error on malformed input.

nodes.name: sortedAny
nodes.summary: Recursive helper that walks a decoded JSON tree and rebuilds it with object keys sorted lexicographically, enabling deterministic canonical encoding.

nodes.name: DriftReport
nodes.summary: Result struct returned by CheckDrift carrying a Detected boolean, the list of triggered criteria (CPU/LLM/Corpus/DateExpired), and a human-readable Details slice.

nodes.name: Env
nodes.summary: Snapshot of the runtime environment's CPU, LLM, and corpus fingerprints used by CheckDrift to compare against the stored Profile.

nodes.name: CheckDrift
nodes.summary: Compares a stored Profile against an Env snapshot and a 'now' timestamp, emitting a DriftReport that flags any of the four criteria: CPU mismatch, LLM mismatch, corpus mismatch, or DateRevision expiry. Empty fingerprints in Env are skipped (test-friendly).

nodes.name: FingerprintCorpus
nodes.summary: Computes the SHA-256 hex digest of a JSONL corpus file, used as a stable identifier of the training/evaluation set that produced a given Profile.

nodes.name: Weights
nodes.summary: Six-field weight vector for D-Sens, D-Authority, D-Invariant and their probe sub-weights, summing to 1.0 by DefaultProfile invariant.

nodes.name: Profile
nodes.summary: Calibration manifest: ID, Version, CreatedAt, DateRevision (anti-pattern #3 péremption guard), VersionMonographie pin, three environment fingerprints, Thresholds, and Weights. Serialized byte-identically by MarshalCanonical.

nodes.name: DefaultProfile
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical default Profile injected by app.NewDispatcher (v0.1.1 P0-02) so the péremption guard is active on the CLI default path. Pins VersionMonographie to chap. III.8 and sets DateRevision two years out.

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: Filesystem-backed Profile store rooted at a directory, exposing versioned Save, Load, LoadCurrent (via current.json pointer), and ExportSHA256 for reproducible-build verification.

nodes.name: Save
nodes.summary: Persists a Profile to disk as canonical JSON under Dir/<id>-<version>.json, then refreshes the current.json pointer (symlink on Unix, file copy on Windows).

nodes.name: refreshCurrent
nodes.summary: Cross-platform pointer update: attempts os.Symlink to current.json, falls back to a copy of the manifest bytes on platforms where symlinks fail (notably Windows without developer mode).

nodes.name: ExportSHA256
nodes.summary: Computes the SHA-256 hex digest of a stored profile file, used by tests and reproducible-build verification to confirm byte-identical serialization across runs.

nodes.name: AtomicThresholds
nodes.summary: Lock-free threshold container holding six sync/atomic.Int64 (milli-encoded float) fields, enabling safe concurrent reads on the dispatcher hot path with occasional atomic updates via SetTuning.

nodes.name: NewAtomicThresholds
nodes.summary: Constructs an AtomicThresholds from a thresholds.Ordered value, panicking if the ordering invariant (deterministe > probabiliste) is violated -- fails fast at boot rather than silently in production.

nodes.name: Snapshot
nodes.summary: Materializes the six atomic thresholds into a value-type thresholds.Ordered snapshot for callers that need a consistent read across multiple fields.

nodes.name: SetTuning
nodes.summary: Atomically updates all six threshold fields from a thresholds.Ordered value, re-validating the ordering invariant and panicking on violation.

nodes.name: CalibrateWeights
nodes.summary: V1 weight calibration hook that returns the base Weights unchanged on empty corpus, otherwise defers to the configured WeightHook (currently a passthrough). Seam reserved for a future learning strategy.

nodes.name: Dispatcher
nodes.summary: Stateful orchestrator holding the LLM client, thresholds, optional Profile, and a clock seam. Its Decide method runs the 8-step pseudo-algorithm and returns a Decision with a ventilated Trace.

nodes.name: NewDispatcher
nodes.summary: Constructs a Dispatcher with an LLM client and ordered thresholds, panicking if thresholds violate the ordering invariant. Initializes the clock to time.Now.

nodes.name: NewDispatcherWithProfile
nodes.summary: Like NewDispatcher but binds a calibration Profile so the dispatcher applies Profile.Weights at step 6 and enforces the DateRevision expiry guard at step 3 (v0.1.1 P0-02).

nodes.name: Decide
nodes.summary: The 8-step decision pipeline (PRD §10): (1) frontier check; (2) D-Authority + verrou ontologique; (3) profile expiry; (4) D-Sens + D-Invariant + I4 coherence; (5-6) composite TauScore via Profile.Weights; (7) regime mapping with hysteresis; (8) I1-I5 evaluation populates Trace. Step order is load-bearing -- not arbitrary.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds Trace.{DSens,DAuthority,DInvariant} *tau.Score at steps 2 and 4 (ADR-0008), then composes them into TauScore at steps 5-6.

nodes.name: refusDecisionWithScores
nodes.summary: Builds a Refus Decision carrying the already-computed ventilated dimension scores (D-Auth, D-Sens, D-Invariant) so the caller can inspect the partial state that triggered refusal.

nodes.name: dimensionWeights
nodes.summary: Returns the (D-Sens, D-Authority, D-Invariant) weight triple from the injected Profile when present, otherwise the package defaults (v0.1.1 P0-02).

nodes.name: DefaultThresholds
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical default thresholds.Ordered value used by NewDispatcher when no calibration Profile is supplied. Aliases the transverse internal/thresholds type per ADR-0006.

nodes.name: adapter_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests pinning the neutral DTO surface of the AgentMeshKafka bridge: JSON round-trip, zero-value safety, omitempty attestation behavior, and the Adapter interface signature (ADR-0005).
nodes.languageNotes: All test functions call t.Parallel() per project convention (CLAUDE.md §Conventions de code).

nodes.name: adapter.go
nodes.summary: Declares the neutral DTO types (AgentMeshExchange, AgentMeshPrincipal, AgentMeshCapability, AgentMeshAttestation) and the streaming Adapter interface that bridges AgentMeshKafka events to the tau kernel per ADR-0004 and ADR-0005.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure type/interface declarations; ADR-0005 mandates that no domain types leak upward through these DTOs.

nodes.name: classifier_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests the I4 empirical classifier: bucket transitions for ClassifyI4, sensitivity/specificity arithmetic in EmpiricalI4Summary, and JSON omitempty semantics when sensitivity is nil.

nodes.name: classifier.go
nodes.summary: Classifies real recorded AgentMeshKafka decisions against the I4 coherence invariant (chap. III.8.5) and aggregates sensitivity/specificity statistics for empirical validation campaigns.
nodes.languageNotes: Bucket model (CoherentAccepted, IncoherentRefused, FalsePositive, FalseNegative, OtherRefusal, Unmodeled) encodes the I4 confusion matrix used to detect anti-pattern #4.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation header for the AgentMeshKafka bridge, citing ADR-0004 (empirical bridge) and ADR-0005 (DTO neutre).

nodes.name: empirical_i4_test.go
nodes.summary: Empirical I4 campaign harness: replays the golden-3.jsonl corpus through the dispatcher, classifies each outcome, hashes the corpus with SHA-256, and emits a versioned JSON report under testdata for regression tracking.
nodes.languageNotes: SHA-256 corpus hash plus profile version anchors the report to a reproducible input set per CLAUDE.md veille active I3.

nodes.name: file_adapter_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests the FileAdapter replay implementation: golden JSONL parsing, context cancellation, idempotent Close, malformed-line resilience, and constructor error on missing files.

nodes.name: file_adapter.go
nodes.summary: File-based replay implementation of Adapter that streams AgentMeshExchange records from a JSONL corpus, with bounded scanner buffer, optional topic filtering, and idempotent shutdown.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses sync.Once for idempotent Close and a child context to ensure goroutine cleanup; channel-based stream pattern matches ADR-0004 bridge contract.

nodes.name: empirical-i4-results.json
nodes.summary: Frozen JSON report from the last empirical I4 campaign run, recording corpus SHA-256, profile version, sensitivity/specificity, and decision counts.

nodes.name: golden-3-malformed.jsonl
nodes.summary: Three-line JSONL fixture with at least one malformed entry, used to verify the FileAdapter continues streaming after parse errors.

nodes.name: golden-3.jsonl
nodes.summary: Three-line JSONL golden fixture of AgentMeshExchange records replayed by the empirical I4 campaign and end-to-end bridge tests.

nodes.name: typography-fr.py
nodes.summary: Script de normalisation typographique française : insère les espaces insécables (U+00A0) avant `: ; ? ! »` et après `«`, applique les guillemets `«…»` aux chaînes droites, et préserve les blocs de code conformément à CLAUDE.md §Conventions éditoriales.
nodes.languageNotes: Préserve les blocs ```...```, le code en ligne `…`, les URLs et les liens markdown via une regex `PRESERVE_RE` appliquée segment par segment.

nodes.name: agentmeshkafka_test.go
nodes.summary: End-to-end pipeline test bridging FileAdapter → app.StreamAsTauExchanges → app.NewDispatcher.Decide over golden and malformed corpora, exercising the full bridge → orchestration round-trip.

nodes.name: calibration_determinism_test.go
nodes.summary: Verifies byte-identical reproducibility of `tau calibrate` over a frozen 200-entry corpus (two runs → identical SHA-256) and that an expired profile (today > date_revision) triggers a refusal on the CLI dispatcher path (anti-pattern #3, P0-02).
nodes.languageNotes: Builds the tau binary into t.TempDir and shells out via exec.Command — the canonical reproducibility gate.

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Package documentation header for the end-to-end test suite spanning bridge, orchestration, and calibration layers.

nodes.name: e2e_trace_test.go
nodes.summary: Asserts that Decision.Trace exposes the ventilated DSens/DAuthority/DInvariant scores end-to-end (ADR-0008) and that custom Profile.Weights propagate through the dispatcher.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Empty placeholder file keeping the tests/calibration/ directory tracked by git for the golden corpus fixture.

nodes.name: golden-corpus.jsonl
nodes.summary: Frozen 200-entry golden corpus driving the calibration determinism test and any reproducible `tau calibrate` regression; modification requires an ADR per CLAUDE.md directive #6.

nodes.name: AgentMeshExchange
nodes.summary: Neutral DTO describing an intent exchange observed on the AgentMeshKafka bus (initiator, target, attestation, source-topic metadata), shielded from domain types per ADR-0005.

nodes.name: AgentMeshPrincipal
nodes.summary: Neutral DTO for an AgentMesh principal carrying ID, organization, human-in-loop flag, and delegation depth — the inputs to I3 D-AUTORITÉ asymmetry checks.

nodes.name: AgentMeshCapability
nodes.summary: Neutral DTO describing a target capability — discovery mode and contract URI — consumed by the dispatcher's D-SENS scoring.

nodes.name: AgentMeshAttestation
nodes.summary: Neutral DTO for an institutional attestation (émetteur, référence, marqueur, assertedAt); absence triggers the I3 ontological refusal lock (Searle 1995).

nodes.name: Adapter
nodes.summary: Streaming interface contract for any AgentMeshKafka source: Stream(ctx, topics) returning channels of exchanges and errors, plus Close. ADR-0004 keeps real Kafka and file replays interchangeable.

nodes.name: EmpiricalDecision
nodes.summary: Flattened view of a tau Decision plus dimension scores and unmodeled observations, supplying ClassifyI4 with the minimal inputs for I4 bucket assignment.

nodes.name: ClassifyI4
nodes.summary: Maps an EmpiricalDecision to one of six I4 buckets (CoherentAccepted, IncoherentRefused, FalsePositive, FalseNegative, OtherRefusal, Unmodeled) by comparing diagnostic, dimension scores, and unmodeled-observation flags.

nodes.name: EmpiricalI4Stats
nodes.summary: Aggregated counters and derived sensitivity/specificity for an I4 empirical campaign; sensitivity and specificity are *float64 with omitempty so absent denominators serialize cleanly.

nodes.name: EmpiricalI4Summary
nodes.summary: Folds a slice of EmpiricalDecision through ClassifyI4, accumulates the six bucket counts, and computes sensitivity/specificity ratios only when the denominators are non-zero.

nodes.name: FileAdapter
nodes.summary: Adapter implementation that streams AgentMeshExchange records from a JSONL file with mutex-guarded lifecycle and a sync.Once stop hook for idempotent shutdown.

nodes.name: Stream
nodes.summary: Opens the file in a child goroutine, returns receive-only channels of exchanges and errors, and registers the cancel function as the stop hook for Close.

nodes.name: scan
nodes.summary: Bounded-buffer JSONL scanner that decodes each line into AgentMeshExchange, applies the optional topic filter (slices.Contains), reports parse errors without aborting, and respects ctx.Done().

nodes.name: Close
nodes.summary: Idempotent shutdown via sync.Once: cancels the child context behind a mutex so concurrent Close calls are safe.

nodes.name: apply_typography_to_segment
nodes.summary: Applique sur un segment de texte les règles typographiques françaises : NBSP avant `: ; ? ! »`, NBSP après `«`, et conversion des guillemets droits en guillemets français appariés.

nodes.name: process_line_outside_code
nodes.summary: Découpe une ligne markdown en segments en préservant le code en ligne, les URLs et les liens, puis applique `apply_typography_to_segment` uniquement hors zones préservées.

nodes.name: process_file
nodes.summary: Traite un fichier complet en gérant les fences ```...``` (passées telles quelles), normalise les fins de ligne, et écrit le résultat sauf en mode `--dry-run`.

nodes.name: collect_targets
nodes.summary: Sélectionne récursivement les fichiers cibles (markdown et `.go` selon liste blanche) sous la racine fournie en évitant les répertoires exclus.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Point d'entrée CLI : parse `--root` et `--dry-run`, collecte les cibles via `collect_targets`, applique `process_file` à chacune, et affiche un résumé des fichiers modifiés.

nodes.name: TestEmpiricalI4Campaign
nodes.summary: End-to-end empirical campaign: hashes the golden corpus, streams it through the dispatcher, builds EmpiricalDecisions, computes EmpiricalI4Summary, and writes a versioned JSON report under testdata/.

nodes.name: TestCalibrationDeterministic
nodes.summary: Runs `tau calibrate` twice with the same canonical inputs and asserts the two output profiles have identical SHA-256 hashes — the byte-identical reproducibility gate.

nodes.name: TestExpiredProfileRefuses
nodes.summary: Constructs a dispatcher whose profile date_revision lies in the past via WithClock, then asserts Decide returns a Refus with the péremption diagnostic — guards anti-pattern #3 on the CLI dispatcher path.

nodes.name: TestCalibrate_GoldenCorpus_FixedHash
nodes.summary: Runs `tau calibrate` once over the frozen 200-entry golden corpus and asserts the resulting profile matches a checked-in SHA-256 — pins reproducibility across releases.

nodes.name: TestE2E_AgentMeshKafka_FullPipeline
nodes.summary: Full happy-path pipeline: FileAdapter on the golden corpus → app.StreamAsTauExchanges with topic filter → app.NewDispatcher.Decide, asserting non-zero decisions and no fatal errors.

nodes.name: TestE2E_AgentMeshKafka_FullPipeline_NoTopicFilter
nodes.summary: Same pipeline as the happy-path test but with an empty topic slice, verifying that all exchanges are forwarded when no filter is specified.

nodes.name: TestE2E_AgentMeshKafka_MalformedCorpus
nodes.summary: Runs the pipeline against the malformed JSONL fixture and asserts the stream surfaces parse errors without aborting and that well-formed lines still reach a Decision.

nodes.name: TestE2E_TauDecide_TraceVentileeEtPoidsProfil
nodes.summary: Builds a synthetic Exchange via testutil helpers, runs it through both default and custom-weighted dispatchers, and asserts Decision.Trace exposes non-nil positive DSens/DAuthority/DInvariant scores (ADR-0008) plus that custom Profile.Weights shift TauScore.

nodes.name: ADR-0001 — Clean Architecture 4 couches
nodes.summary: ADR fondatrice posant la décomposition en quatre couches étanches (cmd/app/tau/bridge) et la garde architecturale exécutable via arch_test.go. Établit l'inversion de dépendance qui isole le cœur tau/ de tout import LLM concret.

nodes.name: ADR-0002 — Go 1.25 toolchain
nodes.summary: ADR fixant la toolchain Go 1.25+ (alignement FibGo) et l'épinglage golangci-lint v1.64.8 avec 24 linters actifs. Justifie le choix par stabilité PGO et support generics éprouvé.

nodes.name: ADR-0003 — LLM client injecté via interface étroite
nodes.summary: ADR définissant l'interface narrow bridge/llm.Client comme unique point de contact entre tau/ et tout LLM concret. Injection effectuée en couche app/ pour préserver l'étanchéité et la testabilité par stub déterministe.

nodes.name: ADR-0004 — AgentMeshKafka comme bridge empirique
nodes.summary: ADR introduisant le bridge AgentMeshKafka avec branche contingence FileAdapter (M4 actif) puis KafkaAdapter (M5+ cible). Permet d'ingérer des traces empiriques sans coupler le cœur tau/ à Kafka.

nodes.name: ADR-0005 — AgentMeshKafka adapter retourne un DTO neutre
nodes.summary: ADR imposant que l'adapter AgentMeshKafka retourne un DTO neutre sans fuite de vocabulaire domaine vers le bridge. Garde l'inversion sémantique : c'est app/ qui mappe le DTO vers Exchange.

nodes.name: ADR-0006 — Types valeur transverses (internal/thresholds)
nodes.summary: ADR créant le package internal/thresholds comme type valeur transverse partagé entre tau/, orchestration/ et calibration/. Brise un cycle d'import potentiel tout en imposant l'étanchéité descendante (thresholds n'importe rien).

nodes.name: ADR-0007 — Hystérèse V1 simplifiée (sans LastRegime)
nodes.summary: ADR simplifiant la zone d'hystérèse V1 en supprimant la mémoire LastRegime pour conserver une décision pure de l'état courant. Documente le compromis : déterminisme par-call vs lissage temporel reporté à V2.

nodes.name: ADR-0008 — Trace ventilée D-SENS / D-AUTORITÉ / D-INVARIANT
nodes.summary: ADR introduisant les champs Decision.Trace.{DSens, DAuthority, DInvariant} *tau.Score pour exposer les scores par dimension. Résout la rupture silencieuse I4 en remplaçant le proxy TauScore par la lecture des scores ventilés.

nodes.name: ADR-0009 — Types d'erreurs typées (internal/errors)
nodes.summary: ADR peuplant internal/errors de types typés (DispatchError, RefusError, CalibrationError) et de sentinels errors.Is-compatibles. Standardise la propagation d'erreur tout en gardant la rétro-compat fmt.Errorf %w.

nodes.name: Calibration adaptative — Algorithme V1
nodes.summary: Spécification exécutable de l'algorithme de calibration V1 : grid search déterministe en milli-unités, score d'accord et tie-break conservateur. Définit la sérialisation canonique byte-identique implémentée par internal/calibration/calibrate.go.
nodes.languageNotes: Document algorithmique long (214 lignes, 17 sections) ; couvre encodage milli-unités, contrat de sortie sérialisée et limites V1.

nodes.name: Algorithme de dispatch τ — V1
nodes.summary: Spécification canonique des 8 étapes du dispatcher τ (frontière, gardes D-AUTORITÉ/péremption/I4, scores, composite, hystérèse, invariants). Source de vérité pseudo-algorithmique pour internal/orchestration/dispatcher.go avec ordre non arbitraire justifié.
nodes.languageNotes: Document central de 407 lignes / 22 sections ; chaque étape porte un marqueur épistémique (Confirmé/Probable/Hypothèse) et un mapping vers le fichier Go cible.

nodes.name: Détection de drift — Algorithme V1
nodes.summary: Spécification des cinq critères PRD §11.4 et de l'algorithme de détection de drift (fingerprints CPU, LLM, corpus). Documente l'intégration au dispatcher implémentée par internal/calibration/drift.go.

nodes.name: 03 — L'opérateur τ
nodes.summary: Renvoi exécutable vers chap. III.8.3 : définition de τ, encodage TauGo, trois propriétés exploitables, frontière de validité et ce que τ ne fait pas. Pose le fondement théorique de internal/tau/operator.go.

III.8.5 (conservation, irreducibility, Searle 1995 authority asymmetry, coherence, conjunctive composition) with evaluator, fuzz targets and seed corpora; dimensions ↔ invariants mutual import is forbidden by arch_test.go.
